title: Abyssal Glow
subtitle: Deep sea bioluminescence
body: In the crushing dark below 1000m, life invents its own light. Anglerfish, jellyfish, ostracods — each a cold lantern in the void.

title: Coral Mind
subtitle: Reef intelligence
body: A coral reef is not a collection of animals — it is a single distributed mind, sensing temperature and light across kilometres.

title: Pressure Wave
subtitle: Submarine acoustics
body: Sound travels five times faster in water than air. Whales can communicate across entire ocean basins using infrasound below human hearing.

title: Thermal Vent
subtitle: Hydrothermal chemistry
body: At 400°C, superheated water blackened with minerals erupts from the seafloor, sustaining life that has never seen sunlight.

title: Salinity Lens
subtitle: Ocean stratification
body: Fresh and salt water do not mix instantly. A halocline is a lens of density — ships navigate it, submarines hide within it.

title: Midnight Zone
subtitle: Mesopelagic biology
body: Between 200m and 1000m, the twilight zone teems with the largest daily migration on Earth — billions of creatures rising at dusk, sinking at dawn.

title: Kelp Cathedral
subtitle: Forest ecology
body: Giant kelp grows 30cm per day in nutrient-rich cold water, building underwater forests that shelter thousands of species from currents and predators.
